name: app-store-optimization
description: Complete App Store Optimization (ASO) toolkit for researching, optimizing, and tracking mobile app performance on Apple App Store and Google Play Store
capabilities: Research keyword volume, competition, and relevance for app discovery
Analyze competitor ASO strategies and identify market gaps
Optimize app metadata with platform-specific character limits
Calculate ASO health scores across multiple performance dimensions
Analyze user reviews for sentiment, issues, and feature requests
Generate localization strategies for global app reach
author: sickn33
securitySummary: All 432 static findings are false positives. The scanner misidentified markdown code fences as shell commands, hash-related ASO terminology as weak cryptography, and variable names containing 'request' as network activity. This is a legitimate App Store Optimization skill with no security concerns.
valueStatement: Mobile app developers struggle to get their apps discovered in crowded app stores. This ASO toolkit provides keyword research, competitor analysis, metadata optimization, and performance tracking to maximize app visibility and downloads on Apple App Store and Google Play.
limitations: Does not provide real-time app store data or rankings
No direct integration with Apple App Store Connect or Google Play Console
Keyword search volume estimates are directional, not actual store data
Does not submit or manage app listings on behalf of users

useCases.targetUser: Mobile app developers preparing new app launches
useCases.title: Pre-Launch App Optimization
useCases.description: New app developers use the skill to research keywords, analyze competitors, and create optimized metadata before submitting to app stores.

useCases.targetUser: Product managers and app marketers improving existing apps
useCases.title: Existing App ASO Audit
useCases.description: Established app owners use the skill to audit their current ASO performance, identify weaknesses, and receive optimization recommendations.

useCases.targetUser: Growth marketers and competitive intelligence analysts
useCases.title: Competitive Intelligence
useCases.description: Marketing teams use the skill to monitor competitor strategies, track keyword gaps, and inform product positioning decisions.

promptTemplates.title: Basic Keyword Research
promptTemplates.prompt: Hey Claude—I just added the "app-store-optimization" skill. Can you research the best keywords for my productivity app? I'm targeting professionals who need task management and team collaboration features.
promptTemplates.scenario: Initial keyword research for a new app

promptTemplates.title: Competitor Analysis
promptTemplates.prompt: Hey Claude—I just added the "app-store-optimization" skill. Can you analyze keywords that Todoist, Asana, and Monday.com are using? I want to find gaps and opportunities for my project management app.

promptTemplates.title: Metadata Optimization
promptTemplates.prompt: Hey Claude—I just added the "app-store-optimization" skill. Can you optimize my app title for the Apple App Store? My app is called "TaskFlow" and I want to rank for "task manager", "productivity", and "team collaboration". The title needs to be under 30 characters.
promptTemplates.scenario: Optimizing app title with character limits

promptTemplates.title: Full ASO Audit
promptTemplates.prompt: Hey Claude—I just added the "app-store-optimization" skill. Can you perform a complete ASO audit for my fitness app? I want to understand my keyword rankings, metadata quality, competitor landscape, and get a comprehensive health score with recommendations.

bestPractices: Research keywords before naming your app - include primary keywords in the title while maintaining brand identity
Use platform-specific character limits as guidance, not strict rules - clarity matters more than keyword stuffing
Update metadata regularly based on performance data and seasonal trends, not just at launch
antiPatterns: Do not stuff keywords into titles or descriptions in a way that harms readability
Do not ignore platform differences - Apple and Google have different optimization requirements
Do not set and forget - ASO requires ongoing monitoring and iteration based on performance data

faq.question: How does this skill get keyword search volume data?
faq.answer: The skill provides estimated keyword metrics based on general market knowledge. For actual search volumes, connect to App Store Connect or Google Play Console analytics.

faq.question: Can this skill submit my app to the stores?
faq.answer: No, this skill generates optimized metadata recommendations. You must manually submit or use platform-specific APIs to update your app listing.

faq.question: Does it work for both Apple App Store and Google Play?
faq.answer: Yes, the skill supports both platforms with platform-specific recommendations for character limits, keyword fields, and optimization strategies.

faq.question: How often should I update my app metadata?
faq.answer: Review and update metadata quarterly or when you release significant features. Major updates warrant new 'What's New' sections.

faq.question: Can I use this for games as well as apps?
faq.answer: Yes, the metadata optimization principles apply to games. The skill includes guidance for game-specific elements like screenshots and preview videos.

faq.question: What is a good ASO health score?
faq.answer: A score above 70/100 is considered good. Scores above 85 indicate excellent optimization. Focus on improving your lowest-scoring category first.
